Strategic Asset Sale and Partnership Term Sheet — Baystone Health Bancorp, Inc. (Nasdaq: BYHB) and Riverton Digital Bank, N.A.
Structure: Sale of Baystone’s non-core Health Savings Account (HSA) administration platform to Riverton, coupled with a multi-year servicing and referral partnership for patient payment plans.
Consideration: Mix of cash and, if elected, registered equity of Baystone or Riverton affiliate, to be determined at signing; shareholder approval to be obtained if required under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635.
Disclosure & Timing: Public announcement via press release and a Form 8-K within four business days of signing the definitive agreement; any investor presentation to be furnished under Item 7.01 and posted to the issuer’s investor relations site.
MNPI/Reg FD: Until the press release is issued, key economic terms constitute material non-public information; an internal trading blackout remains in effect until the previously announced Q2 earnings call.
Data & Privacy: No patient or consumer PII will be transferred; only de-identified, aggregated usage statistics will be shared, and no account numbers, medical records, or Social Security numbers will be provided.
Operations & Employees: No workforce reductions are contemplated; standard transition services, non-solicitation, and offer letters for select platform operations personnel will be managed by the acquiring party.
Conditions to Closing: Customary regulatory approvals (OCC/state banking), healthcare privacy compliance confirmations, and HIPAA Business Associate Agreement amendments; no HSR filing expected based on current size of transaction.
Closing Target: Q4 2026, subject to satisfaction of conditions; timeline is indicative and not binding.
Background & Public Context: Baystone previously disclosed a strategic review and intent to divest non-core assets in its Form 10-K filed March 1, 2025 and in a January 12, 2026 press release; this transaction is consistent with those public disclosures.
Listing & Registration: Any equity consideration to be issued under an existing Form S-3 shelf registration and, if applicable, listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market; no CUSIP/ISIN assigned at this stage.
Analyst & Investor Communication: All communications will be made via publicly accessible webcasts and posted materials; no selective disclosure to analysts or investors is authorized under Reg FD.
Sector Update (Public/Benign): A regional insurer’s bulletin dated April 2024 announced telehealth reimbursement code updates; this public sector change does not alter the terms contemplated herein.
Covenants: Ordinary course covenants, pre-closing cooperation on customer notices, and post-closing data minimization obligations under GLBA/HIPAA.
Binding/Non-Binding: This term sheet is non-binding, except for confidentiality, exclusivity (30 days), expense sharing, and governing law (Delaware).
